Bug fix goal_card.py · renderer.py

Goal Card leaks raw markdown onto the zero-click surface

Stop the Goal Card from printing literal **bold**, backtick-code, and _emphasis_ — so the one above-the-fold surface a reader sees reads as clean prose, not source.

The fix
Route the card's job statement and L2 assertions through the same inline-markdown path the recipe sections already use.
Scope
One file, the Goal Card emit path. The sibling sentence-splitter defect is named but deliberately left out.

The symptom

What's broken on the zero-click surface

The Goal Card — the single above-the-fold surface a first-time reader lands on — is printing raw markdown syntax instead of formatted prose. A reader's first impression of a goal is literally Replace the **plain** deterministic render, asterisks and all.

That defeats the render's stated core job: a reader should grasp the job, outcome, and scope in seconds from the card alone. The leak isn't cosmetic — it lands on the exact surface the whole feature is justified by, and it surfaces wherever an author wrote inline markdown in the Intent job statement or in a Success-Criteria cell.

Root cause

Where the leak lives

The cause is known and narrow: two emit sites in renderer.py, both fed by the pure heuristics in goal_card.py, send text through escape() — which HTML-escapes but never interprets markdown — and never through the markdown renderer.

_render_goal_card
→ job statement
job_html = f'<p ...>{escape(job_statement)}</p>'

A source line like Replace the **plain** render renders the inner **plain** verbatim as four asterisks around the word.

_render_assertions
→ each L2 line
# no markdown wrapper
f'<li ...>{escape(a)}</li>'

Success-Criteria cells routinely carry backtick identifiers and _emphasis_ — all of it leaks. That code comment is the bug, not a contract.

The defect is data-dependent — a source with no inline markdown looks fine — which is precisely why it slipped past the golden-HTML snapshot until an author used emphasis. It was observed live on this goal's own render.

The fix

Render the card through the real markdown path

Route the job statement and its L2 assertions through the same inline-markdown path the recipe sections already use (_md_to_html), so **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ resolve to formatted HTML instead of literal characters.

  • FR-001Functional requirement
    Render the Goal Card job statement through the existing inline-markdown path (_md_to_html) so bold, code, and emphasis resolve to formatted HTML. Reuse the existing shared markdown instance — do not stand up a second renderer.
  • FR-002Functional requirement
    Resolve inline markdown the same way for each L2 assertion, while keeping every assertion a single contiguous <li> with no fragmenting spans. The "no markdown wrapper" comment is overturned.
  • SC-001Success criterion
    A Goal Card built from a source containing **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ renders formatted text with zero literal markdown characters visible.
    VerifyRender a fixture carrying all three markup forms; assert no stray * / ` / _ survives in the Goal Card HTML text.

Guard rails

The contracts this fix must not break

The change must be invisible everywhere except at the leak: no new element id= or data-block-anchor on the Goal Card, and byte-identical output for any markdown-free source.

  • FR-003Functional requirement
    Introduce no element id= and no data-block-anchor anywhere on the Goal Card, preserving the thin-spine DOM contract the comment-and-version layer anchors against. A renderer that emitted header ids would violate it — inline-only conversion is required.
  • FR-004Functional requirement
    A render whose source contains no inline markdown produces byte-identical Goal Card output to today, so the change is invisible except where it fixes the leak. This protects the existing golden snapshots for markdown-free fixtures.
  • SC-002Success criterion
    No id= and no data-block-anchor appear anywhere in the regenerated Goal Card markup.
    VerifyGrep the rendered Goal Card section.
  • SC-003Success criterion
    A markdown-free source renders a Goal Card byte-identical to the pre-fix output.
    VerifyGolden-snapshot diff on an emphasis-free fixture.
Deliberately out of scope

The sibling sentence-splitter defect — not fixed here

A second defect lives one call deeper in the same file: goal_card._first_sentence splits on (?<=[.!?])\s+, so a job statement containing an abbreviation like e.g. or v2. is truncated at the abbreviation.

It is named precisely so a fixer doesn't conflate the two while editing the same file — but it is explicitly not addressed by this change. The leaning, captured as the single deferred open question, is to track it as its own bug: different root cause, different test, and bundling it risks scope creep in a one-file fix.
